description: Adversarial invariant for issue #63: PCR must not become a prerequisite for earlier broad DNA sequencing or recombinant DNA nodes, while PCR-specific workflows remain connected.

checks.reason: Broad DNA sequencing predates PCR and includes non-PCR template routes.
Recombinant DNA was demonstrated before PCR and should not require a later amplification method.
PCR must remain connected to the PCR-specific diagnostic workflow as a positive control.
PCR diagnostics is the narrow workflow where PCR is a hard method dependency.
PCR is an important molecular-diagnostics enabler without becoming a prerequisite for all DNA sequencing.
The recombinant DNA node must remain earlier than PCR.
Broad DNA sequencing must remain earlier than PCR.
PCR should stay anchored to the source-backed 1985 chronology.
